rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant J.S. Guerin & Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (negligence ¿ survival and wrongful death, strict liability ¿ survival and wrongful death, and loss of consortium), granted as to issue 7 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cantley¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Murphy was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hamilton Red Dot joint compound) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ amended responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, Mr. Schutte¿s deposition testimony, Mr. Ay¿s declaration and Mr. Hamilton¿s deposition testimony are sustained. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant San Francisco Gravel Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (negligence ¿ survival and wrongful death, strict liability ¿ survival and wrongful death, and loss of consortium), granted as to issue 7 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cantley¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Murphy was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Gold Bondjoint compound and Monokote fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ amended responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint By Defendant Cahill Contractors Inc., Cahill Construction Company, Inc. And Cahill Construction Services Inc.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendants Cahill Contractors, Inc., Cahill Construction Co., Inc. and Cahill Construction Services, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ third amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint ¿ the summary complaint and Brayton Purcell¿s master complaint ¿ states allegations sufficient to properly plead a claim for punitive damages. The court did not consider the deposition testimony of Darryl Diamond, which both parties attached. The grounds for a motion to strike must appear either on the face of the challenged pleading or from matters which the court may judicially notice. (C.C.P. § 437.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY PUCHOSIC VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories (specifically plaintiff¿s cite to and summary of defendant¿s General Order 129 responses) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Nixt¿s declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY CAMPBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Ford Motor Co.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs plaintiff of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)
